title: Helping investors feel reassured the moment after they invest
summary: I led the redesign of the post-investment experience, translating user research into a clearer, more coherent journey that reduced support demand and rebuilt investor confidence.
outcomes: 30% reduction in support tickets for funding summaries (MoM)
5/5 participants intuitively identified where their investment was located in usability testing
Transparent journey improved user flow and delight

sections.title: Design challenge
body: David has just invested in a company he believes in using his investment app. Checkout completed, payment confirmed.
Curious to see it in his portfolio, he opens the app again but his investment isn't there, like the shares and the money he just committed, have simply vanished.
In this scenario many investors turned to customer support, expecting their new investment to appear immediately in their portfolio.
But the reality was more nuanced - campaigns remain open for further investment, and shares only move into the portfolio once the round closes and due diligence is complete.
Unfortunately, the product didn't communicate this clearly, leaving investors disoriented at the exact moment reassurance was most needed.

media.alt: Investor feedback quotes and mobile chat showing user asking about missing investment in portfolio

sections.title: Action taken
body: User interviews, support ticket analysis, and session replays revealed a consistent issue: investors struggled to find their investment immediately after checkout. Rage clicks told their own story.

The native app had strong business backing, giving us the space to prioritise this opportunity. Our first solution adjusted the flow, taking users from checkout straight into an 'Open Investments' screen, which tested well and reassured them their purchase had gone through.

inlineMedia.text: I saw it straight after checkout, but when I navigated away it felt like I lost it again.

media.alt: Mobile app investment dashboard showing Open Investments and portfolio

sections.title: Key findings
body: Testing was positive. The new flow reassured investors that their purchase had gone through, but once it went live, behaviour told a different story.

Investors saw the confirmation, yet when they tried to find their investment again, the experience felt hollow. Moving the user to “Open investments” made sense to anyone familiar with the product, but not to the majority that expected to see their money in their portfolio. Instead, they saw something unfamiliar, and sometimes missed it entirely.
identifiedItems: Success toasts obscured the back button, interrupting navigation.
The transition from checkout to "open investments" lacked continuity, causing users to lose context.
The term "open investments" did not align with users' expectations or mental models and was frequently overlooked.
The absence of a clear empty state left users without guidance on what to do next.

media.alt: User journey map showing checkout flow, portfolio screens, and identified friction points

sections.title: Further actions taken
body: The breakthrough came when we stopped showing the portfolio until an investment had actually closed.
By reworking the information architecture, clarifying language, and introducing scalable components, we aligned the experience with investor expectations and removed key points of friction.
This approach removed a major source of confusion and established a more trustworthy foundation for the platform.
identifiedItems: Portfolio only displayed once an investment has closed, removing confusion and creating a more meaningful first experience.
Live and closing campaigns are displayed clearly, making progress easier to follow and increasing transparency.
Improved status feedback such as live, closing, % completion give users continuity from funding through to ownership.

media.alt: Investment detail screens with deeper actions and further information modals
Portfolio screens showing in-progress investments and discover businesses modal

sections.title: Benefits
body: As a result of the release, support tickets dropped and follow-up conversations with investors revealed greater confidence after checkout. The release now underpins all mobile investment journeys and is being rolled out across the web product.

media.text: Before, I'd invest and then panic because nothing showed up. Now I can actually see what's happening.

media.alt: Portfolio screens showing empty state, in-progress investments, and closed campaigns

identifiedItems: 30% reduction in support tickets for funding summaries (MoM)
5/5 participants intuitively identified where their investment was located in usability testing
Transparent journey improved user flow and delight
